prompt: Assess Kanban maturity for a software support team that has been using Kanban for 2 years but still struggles with unpredictable lead times and frequent expedite requests.
assertions: Must analyze WIP limit adherence and whether limits are properly calibrated
Must evaluate flow metrics including lead time distribution and throughput stability
Must assess expedite class abuse and its impact on standard work predictability
Must recommend specific continuous improvement actions based on flow data

prompt: Our Kanban board has 6 columns but we never hit our WIP limits because they are set too high. Throughput varies wildly week to week. What is wrong with our system?
assertions: Must identify WIP limits as miscalibrated based on the described symptoms
Must correlate high WIP with throughput variability using Little's Law
Must recommend specific WIP limit reductions with a method for finding optimal limits
Must suggest using control charts or CFDs to diagnose flow problems

prompt: Evaluate our Kanban service classes. We have Standard, Urgent, and Critical. About 40% of items are marked Critical, 35% Urgent, and only 25% Standard.
assertions: Must flag the inverted distribution as a sign of service class misuse
Must assess whether clear classification criteria exist for each service class
Must evaluate the impact of this distribution on overall system flow and SLAs
Must recommend rebalancing with objective classification criteria tied to cost of delay

prompt: We want to measure our Kanban continuous improvement maturity. We do retrospectives monthly but rarely implement the action items. We have no formal Kanban cadences beyond standup.
assertions: Must audit the presence and absence of the seven Kanban cadences
Must identify the gap between generating improvement ideas and executing them
Must assess whether improvement decisions are data-driven or opinion-based
Must provide a maturity score with a roadmap to advance to the next level
